rulings.Case Title: CARL RANSDELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 10, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: CARL RANSDELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 10, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT BW/IP INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DECEDENT IDENTIFIED DEFENDANT'S PUMPS AND THAT GASKETS AND PACKING MATERIALS WERE DISTURBED IN HIS PRESENCE. DEFENDANT'S INTERROGATORY RESPONSES REFLECT SALES BY DEFENDANT OF PUMPS WITH ASBESTOS GASKETS/PACKING IN BOTH THE ORIGINAL EQUIPMENT AND AFTER MARKET. DEFENDANT FAILED TO CLARIFY WHETHER DECEDENT WAS EXPOSED TO ORIGINAL AND/OR AFTER MARKET PRODUCTS. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MEET THE STANDARD SET FORTH IN SCHEIDING V DINWIDDIE CONSTRUCTION CO. (1999) 69 CAL. APP. 4TH 64, 80. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO FRAUD AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS NOT POINTED TO SPECIFIC DISCOVERY RESPONSES THAT DIRECTLY ADDRESS THE ISSUES. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO PARAGRAPH 13 OF THACKER, DENIES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO GERCHOW DECLARATION EXHIBIT A, PAGE 244;21 THRU 245:5 AND DOES NOT RULE ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO AYE DECLARATION. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DOYLE JOHNSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 10, 2007, LINE 2. DEFENDANT GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF UNDER ANDREWS V FOSTER WHEELER (2006) 138 CAL. APP. 4TH 96. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. THE VAILLANCOURT DECLARATION FAILS TO POINT TO ANY FACTS THAT MAY BE DISCOVERED TO SUPPORT OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION; NOR DOES THE DECLARATION EXPLAIN THE DELAY IN DISCOVERING AND/OR SEEKING THIS INFORMATION. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ALVIN RATLIFF VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 10, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANT FRASER'S BOILER SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 5, 2007 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR KLIMACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 10, 2007, LINE 5. DEFENDANT MONTEREY MECHANICAL COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IS NOT SUBJECT TO A MOTION TO STRIKE UNDER D'AMICO BECAUSE IT IS NOT DIRECTLY CONTRARY TO HIS DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AT PAGE 799, PLAINTIFF STATES HE HAD A CONVERSATION WITH A MONTEREY PIPEFITTER. THE TESTIMONY AT 1169-70 DOES NOT EFFECTIVELY PROBE THE DETAILS OF THE CONVERSATION. IT IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO RELY UPON PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY TO NEGATE THE EXISTENCE OF A CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN DEFENDANT AND THERMAL INSULATION (FACT 34). =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: ENVIRONMENTAL WORLD WATCH,INC.,IN THE PUBLIC VS. AMERICAN AIRLINES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order That Former Pltf'S Counsel Yeroushalmi Pay As Sanctions Plt'S Reasonable Atty Fees & Costs For Bringing It'S Mtns For An Order
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 10, 2007, LINE 6. PLAINTIFF ENVIRONMENTAL WORLD WATCH, INC.'S MOTION For Order That Former PLAINTIFF'S Counsel Yeroushalmi Pay As Sanctions PLAINTIFF'S Reasonable ATTORNEY Fees & Costs For Bringing It'S MOTIONS For An Order: Motion for attorneys' fees and costs is granted in the sum of $3,000.00. The authority of the Court to impose sanctions is limited by CCP§ 128.7 and is applicable only to pleadings that fail to meet the standards prescribed by the statute. In awarding sanctions under this provision, the Court may award some or all of the attorneys' fees incurred as a result of the conduct sanctioned. Previously, the Court has described the conduct of Mr. Yeroushalmi. In making this award, the Court finds that the pleadings of April 18, 2007 and May 2, 2007 contain factual representations that fail to meet the standards set forth in CCP § 128.7(b). This award is the total sum applicable to all 4 cases. Although the moving parties' actual fees and costs are well in excess of this sum and appear reasonable under the circumstances, what the Court may award is prescribed by CCP § 128.7. =(302/PJM/JF)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 10, 2007, LINE 11. DEFENDANT HAMILTON MATERIALS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MELVIN HARRIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 10, 2007, LINE 11. DEFENDANT DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT DID NOT REBUT PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORY TWO ASSERTIONS THAT DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS CONTAINED ASBESTOS WHEN PLAINTIFF WORKED WITH THE PRODUCT. C. LEWIS' TESTIMONY WAS NOT ADDRESSED BY DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON THE TESTIMONY OF WELLINGTON AND DOWD ONLY CREATES TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO DECLARATION OF STEPHEN DAVIS AND EXHIBITS N, P, Q, R AND S ARE SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO AYE DECLARATION WAS NOT CONSIDERED BECAUSE DEFENDANT DID NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 10, 2007, LINE 16. DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED.
